content.text: Design Note — SM-1064: Vendor Admin - Upload/Replace Company Logo
Add an "Upload/Replace Company Logo" section to the new Vendor Admin (Angular) Edit and New forms, mirroring the existing functionality in the Old Vendor Admin (CakePHP views).
Backend (CakePHP 1.3): New upload action in vendor_admin_controller.php
Database: No schema changes — logos are stored as files on disk, not in DB
GET /users/vendor_image/{id} — Existing logo retrieval (old admin) — Unchanged, shared storage
ApsVendor (aps_vendor.php): Already has getImagePath($vendor_id) method that resolves to webroot/files/vendor_images/vendor_{id}.png. No changes needed — the new controller can reuse this method.
Validates file type (PNG only per AC2)
Creates directory if it does not exist
Permission: same auth checks as other vendor_admin actions
Streams the vendor logo PNG (or 1px placeholder if none exists)
Sets Content-type: image/png and no-cache headers
getLogoUrl(vendorId: number): string — Returns URL string for logo display

Add upload_logo($id) action: Accept multipart POST with file field, validate file is PNG (check MIME type + extension), save to webroot/files/vendor_images/vendor_{id}.png (same path as old admin), return JSON response with success/error status.
Add get_logo($id) action: Reuse ApsVendor::getImagePath($id) to resolve file path, stream image with proper headers and no-cache directives.
Add uploadLogo(vendorId, file) method using FormData + HttpClient POST
Add getLogoUrl(vendorId) method returning the backend URL
Add new mat-expansion-panel section titled "Upload/Replace Company Logo"

Contents: Image preview showing current logo (or placeholder), hidden file input (accept PNG), "Upload/Replace Company Logo" button triggering the file input, file name display after selection, upload status feedback (success/error snackbar)
On dialog open (edit mode): set logoPreviewUrl to getLogoUrl(vendorId)
On file select: validate PNG type + show client-side preview via FileReader
On upload: call vendorService.uploadLogo(), show snackbar on result
New mode handling: upload logo after initial vendor creation (requires vendor ID)
Shared Storage: Both old and new admin write to the same vendor_images/ directory with the same naming convention, ensuring data consistency (AC3).
New Vendor (Create) Flow: Logo upload requires a vendor ID. For new vendors, the logo section will be available but upload will execute after the vendor is first saved and an ID is assigned.
File Validation: Server-side MIME type validation for PNG files. Client-side accept="image/png" attribute on file input.
No DB Changes: Logo presence is determined by file existence on disk — no database column needed.
Pattern Consistency: Follows the existing upload patterns used by Incident File Upload and Certificate Upload components (FormData, HttpClient, snackbar feedback).

Resolution Summary: Added Upload/Replace Company Logo section to the new Vendor Admin Edit and New forms, mirroring the old Vendor Admin functionality. Users can upload PNG files as company logos. Logos are stored at the same file path used by the old admin, ensuring data consistency between old and new systems.

Backend: Added upload_logo and get_logo endpoints to vendor_admin_controller.php. upload_logo accepts PNG file uploads via multipart/form-data and saves to webroot/files/vendor_images/vendor_{id}.png. get_logo streams the logo image with no-cache headers.

Frontend: Added "Upload/Replace Company Logo" mat-expansion-panel section to vendor-edit-dialog (after Basic Information). Added uploadLogo and getLogoUrl methods to vendor.service.ts. Logo upload is chained into the save flow so new vendors get their logo uploaded after initial creation. Added styles for logo preview, placeholder, and upload controls.
Commits: Backend SVN: r16794 Frontend Git: SM-1064 branch, commit d5b3a1c8, merged to develop

Scenario 1 - Upload logo in Edit mode: Log in, go to Vendor Admin, open an existing vendor for editing, expand "Upload/Replace Company Logo" section, click Upload Logo, select a PNG file, verify preview shows, click Upload Now, verify success snackbar. Reopen the vendor to confirm logo persists.

Scenario 2 - Replace existing logo: Open a vendor that already has a logo, expand the logo section, verify existing logo displays, click Replace Logo, select a different PNG, click Upload Now, verify new logo replaces old one.

Scenario 3 - Upload logo for new vendor: Click Add New Vendor, fill required fields, expand logo section, select a PNG file, note the hint "Logo will be uploaded when vendor is saved", click SAVE, verify vendor is created and logo is uploaded. Reopen to confirm.
Scenario 4 - Cross-admin consistency (AC3): Upload a logo in the new Vendor Admin, then open the same vendor in the old Vendor Admin and verify the logo appears there too (and vice versa).
Scenario 5 - PNG validation: Try uploading a non-PNG file (JPG, GIF, etc.), verify it is rejected with "Only PNG files are allowed" message.
Scenario 1 – Upload logo in Edit mode
you can log in and navigate to Vendor Admin
opening an existing vendor for editing is possible
selecting a PNG file shows a preview
reopening the vendor confirms the logo persists
opening a vendor that already has a logo is possible
expanding the logo section displays the existing logo
selecting a different PNG file is possible
replaces the old logo with the new one
Scenario 3 – Upload logo for new vendor
filling in required fields (Company Name, Status, Address, City, State, Zip, EIN, Primary Contact) is possible
expanding the logo section shows the upload option
selecting a PNG file displays the hint
“Logo will be uploaded when vendor is saved”
creates the vendor and uploads the logo
reopening the newly created vendor confirms the logo persists
uploading a logo in the new Vendor Admin is possible
opening the same vendor in the old Vendor Admin shows the logo there too
uploading a logo in the old Vendor Admin reflects in the new Vendor Admin
attempting to upload a non‑PNG file (JPG, GIF, etc.) is rejected

reopening the vendor confirms the logo persists

Merged and Ready for Stage Testing\!
